title: Health & Emergencies
shortSummary: Describe symptoms, see a doctor, and get help fast in an emergency.
culturalNote: Traditional Chinese medicine (中医) sits alongside Western medicine, and many people use both. Pharmacies (药店) are common and pharmacists can advise on minor problems. In an emergency, the ambulance number is 120, the police is 110, and fire is 119. Hospitals can be busy, so having your symptoms written in Chinese on your phone helps a lot.

keyPhrases.english: I need to see a doctor

grammar.explanation: To say something hurts, name the body part and add 疼 (téng) or 痛 (tòng): 头疼 (headache), 肚子疼 (stomach ache). No verb 'have' is needed.
grammar.commonMistake: Adding 有: it is 我头疼, not 我有头疼.

grammar.explanation: To say you are allergic to something, use 对 (duì, toward) plus the thing plus 过敏: 我对海鲜过敏 (I'm allergic to seafood).
grammar.commonMistake: Dropping 对: the frame is 对 ... 过敏.

dialogue.setup: Mei visits a pharmacy because she is not feeling well.

lines.speaker: 药剂师
lines.english: Hello, where do you feel unwell?

lines.speaker: 小美
lines.pinyin: Wǒ tóu téng, hái fāshāo le.
lines.english: I have a headache and a fever too.

lines.speaker: 药剂师
lines.pinyin: Nǐ duì shénme yào guòmǐn ma?
lines.english: Are you allergic to any medicine?

lines.speaker: 药剂师
lines.chinese: 吃这个药，多休息，多喝水。
lines.pinyin: Chī zhège yào, duō xiūxi, duō hē shuǐ.
lines.english: Take this medicine, rest more, drink more water.

quiz.question: How do you say 'I have a headache'?
What is the ambulance number in China?
quiz.explanation: 120 is the ambulance; 110 is police, 119 is fire.

quiz.question: Which means 'I don't feel well'?
quiz.explanation: 我不舒服 means 'I don't feel well'.
